---
title: "Your AI account: chat, paid, free"
---

# Your AI account: chat, paid, free

::: {.callout-warning}
## Placeholder chapter
Stub. To be drafted via the chapter-writing skill.
:::

## What this chapter will cover

Before we use AI for serious work we need an account. This is a one-time decision that takes ten minutes.

- The frontier providers in this edition: Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI (ChatGPT), Google (Gemini), Mistral.
- The free tier vs. the paid tier — what each one is good for.
- The student angle: many providers and most universities have student programmes; ask before you pay.
- One model is enough for most of the book. The capstone benefits from access to two so you can compare.
- Privacy: what each provider does with your prompts on the free tier. (Short answer: assume non-zero exposure on free tiers; check the policy if your data is sensitive.)

## What this chapter will *not* cover

API keys live in a later chapter (Part VI). You do not need them yet. You can do the entire first six parts of the book with a chat-only account.

## TODO for the author

- A side-by-side of the two main accounts the book uses (Claude Pro, ChatGPT Plus) — features, monthly cost, what changes on the free tier.
- A "what we use in this book and why" paragraph.
- Three pitfalls: credits expiring on free tiers, login methods that surprise students, organisation accounts vs. personal.
